chief_complaint: A 45-year-old female patient presents with a 2-day history of melena and hematemesis.
history: The patient underwent a laparoscopic Roux-en-Y gastric bypass 6 months ago for obesity. She reports a 2-day history of melena and hematemesis. She has no history of gastrointestinal disorders or bleeding. She takes no medications. She has a history of smoking.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 90 bpm, RR 18/min. Physical examination reveals a pale appearance and a soft, non-tender abdomen.
labs: Complete Blood Count (CBC): Hb 8.5 g/dL, MCV 85 fL. Upper GI endoscopy: active bleeding from a small bowel diverticulum in the biliopancreatic limb.
correct_diagnosis: Active bleeding from a small bowel diverticulum in the biliopancreatic limb
differentials: Active bleeding from a small bowel diverticulum in the biliopancreatic limb
Gastrointestinal bleed due to Mallory-Weiss tear
correct_ranking: Active bleeding from a small bowel diverticulum in the biliopancreatic limb
Gastrointestinal bleed due to Mallory-Weiss tear
